violations.text: Cheese was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2025
Drinks were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the chest freezer and bar cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025

Hamburgers are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

At the time of inspection, the PIC stated more time is needed for the Rhode Island State License for Certified Food Safety Manager application to be submitted. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023
